data.purpose: Structured metadata: dates, durations, priorities, timestamps, keyboard hints — always mono per the Mono-For-Data Rule.

shadows.name: modal
shadows.purpose: The one deliberate elevation moment — modals only.

shadows.name: raised
shadows.purpose: Reserved for a future subtle hover-elevation state; unused today (flat-by-default).

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Default easing for all state transitions and modal/view motion.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single most-committed action in a context (Save, Apply schedule).
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; background: oklch(56% 0.21 293); color: white; border: 1px solid oklch(56% 0.21 293); padding: 6px 12px; border-radius: 6px; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; transition: background 120ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), border-color 120ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), transform 120ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(49% 0.21 293); border-color: oklch(49% 0.21 293); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.97); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(56% 0.21 293); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Default action button — surface background, ink text, border shifts to accent on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; background: oklch(96.5% 0.005 290); color: oklch(15% 0.02 290); border: 1px solid oklch(62% 0.015 290); padding: 6px 12px; border-radius: 6px; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; transition: border-color 120ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { border-color: oklch(47% 0.19 293); }

components.name: Chip (accent)
components.description: Structured metadata label — a rectangle, never a pill. Always monospace.
components.css: .ds-chip-accent { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; padding: 2px 7px; border-radius: 4px; font: 500 11px/1.4 ui-monospace, Consolas, monospace; letter-spacing: 0.01em; background: oklch(94% 0.03 293); color: oklch(47% 0.19 293); }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard text/date input — border strengthens and fills with accent on focus.
components.css: .ds-input { border: 1px solid oklch(62% 0.015 290); background: oklch(99% 0.003 290); color: oklch(15% 0.02 290); padding: 6px 10px; border-radius: 6px; font-size: 15px; transition: border-color 120ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); } .ds-input:hover { border-color: oklch(47% 0.19 293); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(56% 0.21 293); outline-offset: -1px; border-color: oklch(56% 0.21 293); }

components.name: Nav Tab
components.description: Shared by the header view-switcher and the Projects tab strip. Active state is a tint fill, not an underline.
components.css: .ds-tab { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 5px; background: transparent; border: 1px solid transparent; color: oklch(46% 0.02 290); padding: 6px 12px; border-radius: 6px; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; transition: background 120ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), color 120ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); } .ds-tab:hover { background: oklch(96.5% 0.005 290); color: oklch(15% 0.02 290); } .ds-tab-active { background: oklch(94% 0.03 293); color: oklch(47% 0.19 293); } .ds-kbd { font-family: ui-monospace, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 600; background: transparent; border: 1px solid oklch(87% 0.008 290); border-radius: 3px; padding: 1px 4px; }

components.name: Task Row Checkbox
components.description: The signature interactive element — an outline square that fills solid with an inverse checkmark on completion, not a native checkbox.
components.html: <button class="ds-check" aria-label="Mark done"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" aria-hidden="true"><rect x="3.5" y="3.5" width="13" height="13" rx="3" fill="currentColor"/><path d="M6.2 10.3l2.4 2.4 5.2-5.6" stroke="oklch(99% 0.003 290)" stroke-width="1.8" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" fill="none"/></svg></button>
components.css: .ds-check { background: none; border: none; padding: 2px; color: oklch(47% 0.19 293); display: flex; cursor: pointer; transition: color 120ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), transform 120ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); } .ds-check:hover { transform: scale(1.08); }

narrative.overview: TaskWiki is built to feel like a precision instrument, not a consumer habit-tracker — dense, legible readouts you scan in a second between other work, controls that respond instantly, and nothing on screen that isn't carrying information. It draws on the same lineage as Linear and Things 3: restrained color, a single clear accent, strong typographic hierarchy through weight and size rather than boxes and shadows, and a dark-mode-native canvas that feels like an editor or terminal rather than a marketing surface. This system explicitly rejects the generic AI-SaaS look — gradient text, cream/sand backgrounds, hero-metric cards, tiny uppercase tracked eyebrows, glassmorphism — and rejects the cute, bouncy consumer to-do app register. There is no audience to persuade here; it's a private instrument for one person, used dozens of times a day, and every visual decision optimizes for speed-to-scan over decoration.
keyCharacteristics: Dense, legible, dark-mode-native, fully correct in light mode
One violet accent carrying identity; status colors used only functionally, never decoratively
Structured metadata rendered in monospace, prose rendered in system sans
Flat by default — elevation exists only for modals
Every interactive element has a visible keyboard focus ring; shortcuts shown as literal kbd chips

rules.name: The Solid Fill Rule
rules.body: Never reuse the text-tint accent/danger/success/warn tokens as a solid button background — accent-solid is a separate, fixed-across-themes token verified against white text in both themes.

rules.name: The One Hue Rule
rules.body: Every neutral carries the same 290° hue at near-zero chroma — there is no separate gray, which keeps a restrained single-accent palette cohesive.

rules.name: The Mono-For-Data Rule
rules.body: If a piece of UI text is a value someone would search, sort, or paste (a date, duration, ID-like status, keyboard key), it's set in mono. If it's a sentence a human wrote, it's set in sans.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Depth comes from the bg/surface background-color step, not from shadows. Shadows are reserved for the one moment something is genuinely floating above the page: a modal.

dos: Do set every piece of structured data in mono
Do use accent-solid (not accent) for any solid button fill with white text
Do keep chips as small rectangles, never pills
Do give every interactive element a visible :focus-visible ring
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion for every transition
donts: Don't use the generic AI-SaaS scaffold: gradient text, cream/sand backgrounds, hero-metric cards, tracked eyebrows, glassmorphism, decorative numbered markers
Don't reach for a cute/bouncy consumer-app register: rounded pastel cards, mascots, celebratory emoji, confetti
Don't use emoji as interface iconography anywhere — every icon is a hand-drawn inline SVG
Don't add a shadow to routine content — shadows are reserved for modals only
Don't use border-left/border-right as a colored accent stripe on any card, callout, or list item
